titleStmt.title: Letter from W. M. Chambers to J. J. Taylor, 22 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W M Chambers

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from W. M. Chambers to J. J. Taylor in which the petition of a Ms Julia Conroy is discussed. Chambers explains how Conroy had asked him to write to Taylor and let him know that he knew her and explain her difficulties. Conroy is trying to obtain compensation for an injury that occurred while she was working in an Asylum.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8753-2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mountmellick May 22nd 1916
J J Taylor Esqr. Mrs. Julia Conroy of
Garroon has sent in
a petition and has
asked me to write
you to say I know
her and she is a
most deserving case and
had been in the
county asylum when
she met with the accident
and received no compensation
hoping you will see
that she is compensated
as her arm is quite
useless owing to the
accident. Your obtServant W M Chambers Pending return of Fo6o
From I.L. Asylums
J.H.
23.V.16
